title: The Destruction of Jerusalem (short version, Pepys 2014)
summary: CME00072 is the repository XML for a University of Michigan manually keyed and proofread electronic text derived from two independently published installments of Rudolf Fischer's edition. The XML omits Fischer's introductory pp. 285-288, encodes the Middle English poem on Archiv 111 (1903), pp. 289-298 and Archiv 112 (1904), pp. 25-45, and retains Fischer's two-paragraph German closing commentary on p. 45. Fischer worked through a Cambridge transcript made by A. Reinke and subsequently collated by B. Kiehl against Cambridge, Magdalene College, Pepys MS 2014, formerly Pepys 37; the poem occupies ff. 23r-35v. Current scholarship identifies this as an imperfect short-version witness of The Destruction of Jerusalem or Titus and Vespasian, not the separate alliterative Siege of Jerusalem and not a one-to-one copy of the Latin or Old English Vindicta Salvatoris. Complete public provider PDFs, OCR, metadata, and scan-data sidecars for Archiv volumes 111 and 112 are cached with the exact encoded runs mapped inside each volume. A complete modern METS comparison-edition PDF and Archiv 108 containing Walther Suchier's actual 1902 review are also cached as downstream supporting sources. Magdalene's named public Pepys Digital Library selection did not include Pepys 2014 when checked; whole-object photography and permission routes are recorded with the target folios kept separate. Utrecht University Library also lists a requestable positive microfilm, M121, but the public inventory does not establish the reel's extent or reproduction terms.
review_notes: Fischer's source-edition title Vindicta Salvatoris is retained at the edition layer; the current work title is a later authority decision.

The poem contains sustained Christian anti-Jewish polemic, collective blame, supersessionism, graphic violence, torture, and dehumanization. Descriptive language records those features without endorsing them.
Pepys 2014's manuscript production date and Northamptonshire origin are witness facts, not composition facts for the wider romance or short recension.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00072
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: Internal identifiers IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID are all CME00072.
The 2016-09-08 TEMPHEAD revision describes structure review and proofing but does not provide an item-level production history.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item CME00072
entities.description: A public manually keyed and proofread electronic text selecting the poem text from Fischer's two serial installments and retaining his brief German closing commentary while omitting the 1903 introduction.

entities.label: Rudolf Fischer, Vindicta Salvatoris, first installment (1903)
entities.description: The independently published first installment, containing Fischer's title, description of Pepys MS 2014, source analysis, editorial method, production credits, and poem lines 1-1030. The XML begins only at the poem on p. 289 and omits the introduction on pp. 285-288.

bibliographic.citation: Rudolf Fischer, 'Vindicta Salvatoris. Mittelenglisches Gedicht des 13. Jahrhunderts, zum erstenmal herausgegeben,' Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 111 (1903), 285-298.
bibliographic.title: Vindicta Salvatoris. Mittelenglisches Gedicht des 13. Jahrhunderts, zum erstenmal herausgegeben
bibliographic.container_title: Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen

notes: Printed p. 285 follows the preceding article on a new page; p. 298 ends with Fortsetzung folgt.
The subtitle's thirteenth-century claim is an edition-layer historical assertion, not current witness dating.

entities.label: Rudolf Fischer, Vindicta Salvatoris, second installment (1904)
entities.description: The independently published concluding installment, continuing the poem with lines 1031-3114 on pp. 25-45 and adding Fischer's two-paragraph German closing commentary at the bottom of p. 45.

bibliographic.citation: Rudolf Fischer, 'Vindicta Salvatoris. Mittelenglisches Gedicht des 13. Jahrhunderts, zum erstenmal herausgegeben, II. (Schluss),' Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 112 (1904), 25-45.
bibliographic.title: Vindicta Salvatoris. Mittelenglisches Gedicht des 13. Jahrhunderts, zum erstenmal herausgegeben, II. (Schluss)
bibliographic.container_title: Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen

notes: Printed p. 25 begins immediately after the preceding article; p. 45 contains line 3114 and the complete closing commentary before the following article begins on p. 46.

entities.label: A. Reinke's working transcript of Pepys MS 2014
entities.description: The working manuscript transcript made at Cambridge for Fischer's edition. It is classified provisionally as a manuscript_witness because the schema has no dedicated working-transcript type; it is a modern intermediary, not a medieval textual witness.

notes: Fischer does not state its support, extent, present repository, or whether Kiehl's collation was entered directly on it.
Do not flatten the production route into a direct Fischer-to-codex transcription without acknowledging Reinke's intermediary copy.

entities.label: Cambridge, Magdalene College, Pepys MS 2014
entities.description: The parchment miscellany that supplied Fischer's only continuous base text. The short-version Titus and Vespasian occupies ff. 23r-35v between the Chronicle of Popes and Emperors and Robert of Gloucester's Metrical Chronicle.

date_statements.label: current specialist date
date_statements.value: end of the 14th or start of the 15th century

holding.notes: The College's current catalog guide says there is no online Pepys Library catalog; the exact public component description used here is a University of York scholarly database record, not a holding-institution catalog URL.

notes: Current specialist description localizes production to Northamptonshire.
Contents recorded by the Database of Middle English Romance: Chronicle of Popes and Emperors, ff. 1r-20v; target poem, ff. 23r-35v; Robert of Gloucester's Metrical Chronicle, ff. 36r-62v.
The target is imperfect in 3,114 lines. The database says the opening 814 lines are lacking; Fischer says the first 812 verses are absent.

Fischer reports two ruled blank leaves after the first chronicle and later seventeenth-century headings over the target; full current codicology awaits the printed Pepys catalog or repository confirmation.

entities.label: Utrecht University Library positive microfilm M121 of Pepys MS 2014
entities.description: A positive microfilm listed in Utrecht's official Index to Microfilms of Medieval English Texts. The inventory names Pepys MS 2014 and its Titus and Vespasian and Robert of Gloucester contents but does not state the reel's image count, physical extent, generation, or completeness.

notes: Coverage is unknown until Utrecht confirms whether M121 reproduces the complete codex or only selected textual portions.

notes: The cache retains the whole exposed volume, not a cropped article. No claim is made for unexposed binding exteriors or preservation masters.

entities.label: Database of Middle English Romance records for Titus and Vespasian and Pepys MS 2014
entities.description: Current specialist work and manuscript descriptions identifying IMEV 1881, the broad work date/place/form, modern manuscript census, and the exact Pepys short-version locus and condition.

entities.label: McShane and Wright, The Destruction of Jerusalem, or Titus and Vespasian (2021; digital 2024)
entities.description: A later critical and contextual edition of the long version, used here for current title, genre, source, version, manuscript-census, and antisemitism evidence. It is not an ancestor of Fischer's edition or CME00072 and does not reproduce the Pepys short-version text as its base.

bibliographic.citation: Kara L. McShane and Mark J. B. Wright, eds., The Destruction of Jerusalem, or Titus and Vespasian (Kalamazoo, MI: Medieval Institute Publications, 2021; METS digital edition, 2024).
bibliographic.title: The Destruction of Jerusalem, or Titus and Vespasian

notes: The live Database of Middle English Romance page lists this modern edition as 2022. The official METS page and inspected title page say 2021, with digital publication in 2024, so those primary bibliographic dates are preferred while the catalog discrepancy is retained.

entities.label: Walther Suchier's review of Bergau's Vengeaunce study (1902)
entities.description: The actual Archiv review of Fritz Bergau's 1901 dissertation, published in volume 108, pp. 199-206. It analyzes multiple sources of the wider Vengeaunce poem and distinguishes the alliterative Siege. Fischer's 1904 commentary names Suchier but prints volume/page coordinates that instead point back to Fischer's own first installment.

bibliographic.citation: Walther Suchier, review of Fritz Bergau, Untersuchungen über Quelle und Verfasser des mittelenglischen Reimgedichts: The vengeaunce of goddes deth (The bataile of Jerusalem), Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 108 (1902), 199-206.
bibliographic.title: Review of Fritz Bergau, Untersuchungen über Quelle und Verfasser des mittelenglischen Reimgedichts: The vengeaunce of goddes deth
bibliographic.container_title: Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen

entities.label: Internet Archive complete provider scan of Archiv volume 108 (1902)
entities.description: The complete 510-page grayscale PDF exposed for Internet Archive item archivfrdasstu108brauuoft, cached with complete-volume OCR and metadata/scan-data sidecars to audit the Suchier review and Fischer's malformed citation.

notes: This supporting volume does not contain the Pepys short-version text. Its local copies therefore have no work_portion.

entities.reproduction_format: Grayscale PDF page images with OCR and XML sidecars

scope.notes: Matching identifiers, structure, text, and revision metadata support derivation; the exact transfer event into the git submodule is undocumented.

object.description: Poem text only; Fischer's introduction is omitted

scope.notes: The complete installment occupies pp. 285-298. CME00072 starts with the poem on p. 289, omitting Fischer's manuscript, source, and editorial introduction on pp. 285-288.

subject.description: Continuation through line 3114 and German closing commentary

scope.notes: The XML's unnumbered PB immediately before line 1031 represents printed p. 25; subsequent PB values run 26-45. The complete p. 45 commentary is retained.

object.description: Corresponding opening of Reinke's working transcript

scope.notes: Fischer explicitly says Reinke copied the manuscript at Cambridge for the edition; the transcript's current physical location is unknown.

object.description: Corresponding continuation of Reinke's working transcript

scope.notes: The closing German commentary is Fischer's authorship and is not attributed to Reinke's manuscript transcript.

subject.description: Working transcript of the target poem

scope.notes: Fischer says the manuscript was copied at Cambridge by A. Reinke. The transcript did not reproduce the complete physical Pepys codex.

subject.description: Reinke working transcript as checked for Fischer's edition

scope.notes: Fischer says the manuscript was first copied by Reinke and then collated by B. Kiehl. This relation keeps Kiehl's codex check distinct from Reinke's initial transcription; the wording does not establish whether the collation survives as a separate sheet or annotations on the transcript.

object.description: P witness of the short version

scope.notes: The modern edition uses a different long-version base manuscript; it is collateral authority evidence, not an upstream textual source.

Fischer names Suchier and a review but prints Archiv CXI, 285-298, which are Fischer's own 1903 coordinates. Inspection identifies the actual review at Archiv 108, 199-206. The review does not mention Pepys 2014, so the source of Suchier's claimed recognition of the Pepys text remains unresolved.

subject.description: Review images inside complete volume scan

scope.notes: The complete cached volume is a downstream supporting source with no target-text overlap and therefore no local-copy work_portion.

access.access_method: Read and search the electronic text through the stable U-M item URL; the collection also advertises bulk XML download.

Email Utrecht University Library at library@uu.nl and request consultation or reproduction information for positive microfilm M121, Cambridge, Magdalene College, MS PL 2014. Ask for the reel inventory, first and last manuscript images, target folio mapping, consultation format, and any digital-supply and reuse terms.

access.access_method: Read the exact specialist manuscript component description and linked work record.
notes: This exact component route is scholarly rather than an online holding-institution catalog; Magdalene's current guide states that no Pepys Library online catalog exists.

access.access_method: The current official Pepys Digital Library's enumerated digitized-manuscript selection was checked for shelfmark 2014.
notes: The page enumerated Pepys 878, 911, 1047, 1236, 1307, 1661, and a selected portion of 2981; it did not list Pepys 2014.
This is a dated negative for that named public selection, not proof that no internal, earlier, or future photographs exist. No manuscript images were cached.

access.access_method: Contact the Pepys Librarian in advance with credentials and the exact shelfmark. The Library reports limited research service under alternative arrangements during restoration.

local_copies.label: Magdalene Pepys Library catalogues and research tools guide
notes: Complete supporting access document; this is not a manuscript facsimile.

local_copies.label: Magdalene Pepys Library guidelines for readers
notes: Complete supporting access document; this is not a manuscript facsimile.

access.access_method: Email the Pepys Librarian to request photographic services and separately ask about publication permission, formats, completeness, cost, and reuse terms.
notes: Request the entire publicly obtainable physical object rather than only the work: front and back binding boards/covers, spine, exposed edges when offered, turn-ins and pastedowns, all flyleaves/endleaves and blanks, and every manuscript leaf including adjacent Chronicle of Popes and Emperors and Robert of Gloucester material. Identify target ff. 23r-35v separately for work mapping.
Ask for a repository-confirmed image inventory and foliation crosswalk before describing a delivered set as complete.
Purchase or private-study permission does not authorize Git redistribution, publication, or for-profit print-on-demand use; the College separately requires publication permission.

access.access_method: Read the current digital edition and download its complete PDF through the official edition page.

This edition prints the long version from another base manuscript and has zero continuous target-text overlap with the Pepys short-version source; the introduction describes P, so no work_portion is asserted.
Cached for private research under METS individual-use terms; do not redistribute the PDF.

rights.component: any repository-supplied photographs or facsimile of Pepys MS 2014

notes: A private research copy, if supplied, must remain gitignored unless the College grants redistribution permission in writing.
Publication, repository redistribution, and for-profit or nonprofit print-on-demand use require separate item-specific confirmation; delivery alone is not authorization.

notes: Do not infer permission to digitize, redistribute, publish, or use M121 for print-on-demand from the existence of a consultation route.

rights.component: downloadable modern-edition PDF and digital editorial text
rights.basis: METS says digital editions are for individual use only and directs users to contact the Executive Director to reprint digital text; XML is separately licensed CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.
notes: The cached PDF is a private research aid and must not be committed or redistributed.
The XML license does not automatically license the downloadable PDF or authorize commercial use.

rights.basis: The review was published in 1902 in the complete Archiv volume exposed by Internet Archive.
notes: Copyright status outside the United States was not separately assessed.

editorial_practices.description: Fischer says abbreviations were resolved and the supplied expansion letters marked by italic type. CME00072 represents those spans with HI/@REND='italic'.
editorial_practices.scope: Complete edited poem across both installments

editorial_practices.source_feature: Inconsistent initials and occasional large manuscript initials
editorial_practices.representation: Initial usage regularized; paragraphs supplied; manuscript large initials printed in bold
editorial_practices.description: Fischer says he regularized initial letters and prescribed paragraphing; large manuscript initials already marked some divisions and were printed in bold.
editorial_practices.scope: Complete edited poem across both installments

editorial_practices.source_feature: Continuous manuscript verse requiring editorial line division and numbering
editorial_practices.representation: Verses divided and numbered by the editor
editorial_practices.description: Fischer states that verses had to be divided and numbered. The modern XML's 3,114 L elements and MILESTONE numbering inherit this editorial lineation rather than a photographed manuscript layout.
editorial_practices.scope: Complete edited poem across both installments
notes: The schema has no dedicated lineation practice, so punctuation_normalization is used provisionally for Fischer's explicit textual segmentation.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Reinke's working transcript and the original Pepys manuscript
editorial_practices.representation: Post-transcription manuscript collation by B. Kiehl
editorial_practices.description: Fischer separately credits Reinke with copying and Kiehl with the following collation; the surviving physical form of the corrections is unknown.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two independently published Fischer installments: 1903 pp. 285-298 and 1904 pp. 25-45
editorial_practices.representation: 1903 introduction omitted; poem text pp. 289-298 and full 1904 continuation/commentary pp. 25-45 encoded as one item
editorial_practices.description: The encoding silently joins the two volume runs and retains the German closing commentary. Its 31 PB elements cover the ten-page opening run and twenty-one-page continuation run.

editorial_practices.representation: Manual keying with in-house proofreading/editing and basic TEI semantics
editorial_practices.description: The collection-level production statement applies to the 2016-2018 growth batch that includes this item's 2016 revision, but no item-level keyer or proofreader is named.

evidence.summary: Official stable item route for CME00072; identifies Rudolf Fischer, Braunschweig, George Westermann, 1903-1904, public electronic text, and No Copyright-United States rights context.

evidence.summary: Describes 2016-2018 manual keying from public-domain editions, in-house proofreading/editing, basic TEI semantics, bulk XML availability, public access, and public-domain/No Copyright-United States collection rights.

evidence.citation: Rudolf Fischer, 'Vindicta Salvatoris,' Archiv 112 (1904), complete Internet Archive volume scan
evidence.summary: Direct inspection verifies volume title page and contents, complete continuation from lines 1031-3114, exact endpoint, two-paragraph commentary, partial-version classification, 812-line statement, historical titles, witness count, and malformed Suchier citation.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata and scandata for archivfrdasstu112brauuoft
evidence.summary: Confirms item identifier, volume 112, publisher, source institution, 512 scanned pages, derivative filenames/sizes, Not In Copyright designation, and no-visible-copyright-notice evidence; cached scandata maps printed and BookReader pages.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Titus and Vespasian
evidence.locator: IMEV 1881; composition; form; plot; manuscripts; modern editions
evidence.summary: Identifies the romance, dates composition 1375-1400 in London, describes its rhyming couplets and subjects, lists twelve current manuscript witnesses, identifies Fischer as using Pepys 2014, and lists the McShane-Wright modern edition as 2022 rather than the official edition's 2021 title-page date.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Cambridge, Magdalene College, MS Pepys 2014
evidence.locator: date, place, material, dimensions, and contents; Titus and Vespasian ff. 23r-35v
evidence.summary: Confirms current shelfmark, late-fourteenth/early-fifteenth-century date, Northamptonshire origin, parchment support, 268 x 175 mm dimensions, adjacent contents, exact target locus, 3,114-line extent, imperfection, and stated loss of opening 814 lines.

evidence.citation: McShane and Wright, The Destruction of Jerusalem, or Titus and Vespasian, official METS edition page and PDF
evidence.summary: Confirms title, anonymous author, editors, publisher, print/digital dates, Middle English poetry, romance/hagiographic/Vengeance categories, complete public PDF, and the edition's explicit contextualization of antisemitism, Christian nationalism, and violence.

evidence.citation: McShane and Wright, Introduction to The Destruction of Jerusalem
evidence.locator: title rationale; versions; sources; antisemitism; Manuscripts and Editions
evidence.summary: Distinguishes short and long versions and the separate alliterative Siege, gives P as Pepys 2014 formerly 37 at ff. 23r-35v, says Fischer used P as base, lists short and long witnesses, and explains the current editorial title and ideological context.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, Copyright and Citation
evidence.summary: States that digital editions are for individual use only, reprinting digital text requires contact with the Executive Director, and XML is separately licensed CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.

evidence.citation: Magdalene College, Pepys Digital Library
evidence.locator: Digitised Manuscripts; Publishing and permissions; contact
evidence.summary: The current named selection enumerates Pepys 878, 911, 1047, 1236, 1307, 1661, and selected pages from 2981 but not Pepys 2014; it states that images are copyrighted, publication images must be purchased, permission is required, and photographic enquiries go to the Pepys Librarian.

evidence.citation: Magdalene College, Pepys Library Information for Researchers
evidence.summary: Requires advance contact with credentials and exact items, reports limited alternative arrangements during restoration, prohibits reader photography in its guidelines, offers a photographic service, and gives pepyslibrary@magd.cam.ac.uk for appointments, photography, and enquiries.

evidence.citation: Magdalene College, Catalogues and Research Tools for the Pepys Library
evidence.summary: States that there is currently no online Pepys Library catalog and directs researchers to the multi-volume printed catalog, including volume V.i for medieval manuscripts.

evidence.citation: Utrecht University Library, Index to Microfilms of Medieval English Texts, M121, Cambridge, Magdalene College, MS PL 2014.
evidence.locator: collection introduction, PDF p. 1; M121 entry, printed p. 161 / PDF p. 162
evidence.summary: The official 177-page inventory says the microfilm collection moved to Utrecht University Library in October 2014, directs consultation requests to library@uu.nl, and lists positive microfilm M121 for Pepys MS 2014 with Titus and Vespasian and Robert of Gloucester. It does not state the reel's image count, photographed extent, completeness, or reuse terms.

evidence.citation: Walther Suchier, review of Bergau, Archiv 108 (1902), 199-206, complete Internet Archive volume scan
evidence.locator: printed pp. 199-206 / PDF pages 213-220 / BookReader leaves n223-n230
evidence.summary: Direct inspection confirms the exact review coordinates and Suchier's source analysis of the wider Vengeaunce, including Latin Vindicta, French Venjance, Legenda Aurea, and other sources, while distinguishing the alliterative Siege. The review does not name Pepys 2014.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata and scandata for archivfrdasstu108brauuoft
evidence.summary: Confirms item identity and complete provider derivative files; cached scandata maps Suchier's printed pp. 199-206 to BookReader leaves n223-n230.
notes: Cached scan-data SHA-256: abbc512039f61e05b241a4422e8ecfc7c3586fa97782af6e1c5427c9b9149461. Cached OCR SHA-256: b438cf79b41b604a52e180ad45d83bee279d920fbcabb4f119c697d2add85145.

evidence.citation: Local source-cache integrity, page-range, and hard-link audit for CME00072
evidence.locator: sixteen cached files; complete Archiv 108/111/112 PDFs, OCR, metadata, and scandata; complete METS PDF; two complete Magdalene access guides; complete Utrecht microfilm inventory
evidence.summary: Verified the original thirteen research-cache files' byte counts, SHA-256 values, PDF signatures/page counts, exact article/page mappings, and identical inode numbers between worktree and canonical cache paths. Three subsequently added official supporting PDFs are separately verified by their local-copy records. No Pepys manuscript images were downloaded because no public complete digital object was found; Utrecht M121 is requestable but its physical coverage remains unknown.

open_questions.question: Can Magdalene College supply a complete practical-resolution image set for Pepys MS 2014, and under what private-use, redistribution, publication, and commercial terms?
next_steps: Email pepyslibrary@magd.cam.ac.uk requesting the full physical object—front/back binding boards or covers, spine, exposed edges when offered, turn-ins and pastedowns, all flyleaves/endleaves and blanks, and every manuscript leaf—with target ff. 23r-35v mapped separately.

Ask for a repository-confirmed image count, foliation/inventory, resolution/color profile, delivery format, cost, private-storage permission, Git redistribution terms, publication permission, and commercial/noncommercial print-on-demand terms before any use beyond private research.

open_questions.question: Does Utrecht positive microfilm M121 reproduce the complete physical Pepys MS 2014, and can Utrecht supply or permit a digital surrogate under terms compatible with private research, public Git reference, publication, or print-on-demand use?
next_steps: Email library@uu.nl for the M121 reel inventory, first and last manuscript images, image count, generation, target ff. 23r-35v mapping, consultation options, cost, and any digital-delivery route.
Request written terms separately for private storage, public redistribution, publication, and commercial or noncommercial print-on-demand; do not infer them from consultation availability.

open_questions.question: What is the current holding-institution codicological description and full physical extent of Pepys MS 2014?
next_steps: Consult McKitterick and Beadle, Catalogue of the Pepys Library, volume V.i, or ask the Pepys Librarian for the MS 2014 entry and any later corrections.
Do not promote Fischer's 1903 paleography or ownership description into current catalog facts without confirmation.

open_questions.question: Do A. Reinke's working transcript and B. Kiehl's collation record survive, and were Kiehl's corrections entered directly on the transcript or kept separately?
next_steps: Search Fischer, Brandl, Reinke, and Kiehl papers and ask the Pepys Library whether production correspondence or copies accompanied the edition.
Retain the transcript and collation as distinct evidenced production steps until the physical records are located.

open_questions.question: How should Fischer's statement that 812 opening verses are absent be reconciled with the current specialist record's 814-line figure?
next_steps: Align Pepys line 1 against the current long-version edition and review Phyllis Moe's short/long version study; preserve both published counts until their lineation systems are reconciled.

open_questions.question: Where did Walther Suchier identify the Pepys text as a partial version of the wider Vengeaunce, given that Fischer's printed review coordinates are wrong and the actual 1902 review does not mention Pepys 2014?
next_steps: Check correspondence, later notes, Bergau's complete work, and other Suchier publications; cite Archiv 108, pp. 199-206 only for the source study actually present there.

open_questions.question: What item-level U-M production record explains the choice to omit Fischer's pp. 285-288 introduction while retaining the p. 45 German commentary?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections for item-level keying, proofreading, source-copy, and selection notes; retain the directly observed page alignment meanwhile.
